source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ACB=90^\circ$, $\angle B=30^\circ$, $CD$ is the height on the hypotenuse $AB$, $AD=3$, then what is the length of $BD$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since CD$\perp$AB and $\angle$ACB=$90^\circ$,\\
it follows that $\angle$ADC=$90^\circ=\angle$ACB,\\
since $\angle$B=$30^\circ$,\\
it follows that $\angle$A$=90^\circ-\angle$B$=60^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle$ACD$=90^\circ-\angle$A$=30^\circ$,\\
since AD$=3$,\\
it follows that AC$=2$AD$=6$,\\
thus AB$=2$AC$=12$,\\
hence BD$=AB-AD$=$12-3$=$\boxed{9}$

this is not multiple choice and no direct formula target is given, so the solver must combine conditions
verifiability: the final answer is a single numeric value ('9'), which is easy to check against the geometry constraints
the provided answer_text gives an explicit reasoning chain that can be audited step by step

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ACB=90^\circ$, $\angle B=30^\circ$, $CD$ is the height on the hypotenuse $AB$, $AD=3$. What is the length of $BD$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: Since $CD\perp AB$ and $\angle ACB=90^\circ$, it follows that $\angle ADC=90^\circ=\angle ACB$. Since $\angle B=30^\circ$, it follows that $\angle A=90^\circ-\angle B=60^\circ$. Thus $\angle ACD=90^\circ-\angle A=30^\circ$. Since $AD=3$, it follows that $AC=2AD=6$. Thus $AB=2AC=12$, hence $BD=AB-AD=12-3=\boxed{9}$.
normalization_notes: Preserved the explicit figure reference because the sample is flagged as requiring the image.
Lightly cleaned punctuation and formatting while keeping the original mathematical content and solution logic.
